mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost corporate earnings and drive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.5% are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth, or pairwise correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals improving manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors price in stronger corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers expectations of monetary tightening, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over 60 trading days as higher discount rates weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets price in reduced corporate earnings and fiscal stimulus expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when GDPC1 falls below 2% or pairwise correlation between GDPC1 and returns drops below -0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k and declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days via improved consumer confidence and corporate hiring.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when both PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 5% or interaction term between proxies and returns is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in FEDFUNDS above 2% signals tightening monetary policy, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over 20 trading days as higher borrowing costs dampen equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.
